region_of_origin: United States (Subway global chain, Singapore market)
food_subgroup: Catering sandwich platter — mixed fillings (egg mayo, tuna, chicken ham, chicken BLT, roasted chicken breast)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a multi-variety catering sandwich platter. FOODON_00002473 (sandwich food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Sandwiches) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for a mixed catering platter of multiple sandwich varieties.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept exists for a commercial catering sandwich platter product.

serving.description: 1 portion (1 of 15 cut pieces from 5 footlongs)
serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately one cut portion (roughly 120 g) from the platter, which contains 15 portions cut from five footlong sub rolls.

nutrient_source.title: Subway Sandwiches — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods; supplemented with USDA SR Legacy data for sub roll and mixed fillings composite estimate
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Branded Foods and SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA entry covers this exact five-variety platter. Values are estimated as a weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central Branded Food entries for Subway 6-inch and footlong sandwiches with comparable fillings (egg mayo, tuna, chicken, BLT variants), normalised to per-100 g. USDA FDC NDB entries used as anchors: Subway Tuna 6-inch (FDC ID 2097512), Subway Roasted Chicken (FDC ID 2097510), Subway BLT (FDC ID 2097505), and generic egg-mayo sandwich (SR Legacy 21052). Values represent a plausible central estimate for this mixed platter and are flagged medium confidence.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; sub sandwich / mixed-grain bread composite reference
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Subway Favourites Catering Platter. GI of 53 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white-wheat sub roll sandwiches with mixed protein fillings (GI range 45–56 for comparable Subway-style sandwiches as reported in Atkinson et al. 2021 and Foster-Powell et al. 2002). The dominant glycemic driver is the wheat sub roll; protein and fat fillings moderate the overall GI. A midpoint value of 53 is used. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (53 × 30.7) / 100 ≈ 16.3, rounded to 16.2.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; estimated ~624 mg per portion)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this platter due to its high sodium content from processed meats, mayonnaise, and bread; a single portion may contribute approximately 25–30% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (53) and moderate carbohydrate load from wheat sub rolls
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be mindful of portion size as the wheat sub roll contributes a moderate glycemic load (~16 per portion); pairing with high-fibre vegetables and limiting to one portion can help manage postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate saturated fat from processed meat fillings and mayonnaise
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the combination of high sodium (~520 mg/100 g) and saturated fat (~1.9 g/100 g) from processed chicken ham, egg mayo, and BLT fillings warrants moderation; choosing lower-sodium fillings and limiting platter portions is advisable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Subway Favourites Catering Platter is a commercially prepared catering product sold by Subway restaurants in Singapore, designed for group events such as office meetings and family gatherings. It consists of five footlong submarine sandwiches — each filled with one of five crowd-favourite varieties: Egg Mayo, Tuna, Chicken Ham, Chicken BLT (chicken, bacon, lettuce, tomato), and Roasted Chicken Breast — cut into 15 equal portions of approximately 4 inches each, yielding roughly 120 g per piece. The sandwiches are assembled on Subway's standard Italian or white wheat sub rolls and dressed with vegetables and condiments including lettuce, tomato, and mayonnaise. The platter is intended to serve 10 to 15 people. Nutritionally, each portion provides approximately 218 kcal per 100 g, with a moderate protein content (~11.5 g/100 g) from the varied meat and egg fillings, moderate carbohydrate (~25.6 g/100 g) primarily from the wheat bread, and moderate fat (~7.8 g/100 g) largely from mayonnaise-based fillings. Sodium content is notably high (~520 mg/100 g) due to processed meats and condiments, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 53 (low-moderate range), driven by the wheat sub roll but moderated by protein and fat co-ingestion. The platter is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten or fish allergies. It is halal-certified at Subway Singapore outlets. This product is relevant for clinical dietary counselling contexts involving sodium restriction, carbohydrate management in Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular risk reduction.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry covers this exact five-variety catering platter. Nutrient values are estimated as a weighted composite from multiple USDA FoodData Central Branded Food entries for comparable Subway sandwich varieties, normalised to per-100 g. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for comparable sub-roll sandwiches. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component foods are individually well-documented in USDA FDC and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central Branded Food entries for individual Subway sandwich varieties (Tuna, Roasted Chicken, BLT, Egg Mayo, Chicken Ham) and normalised to per-100 g; no single USDA entry covers this exact platter. 2. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not consistently reported across the USDA Branded Food entries used for this composite estimate. 3. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA Branded Food source entries used. 4. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food entries for these Subway sandwich varieties. 5. GI of 53 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for comparable wheat sub-roll sandwiches with mixed protein fillings; no direct GI measurement for this specific platter exists. 6. Halal status reflects Subway Singapore's general halal certification; individual outlet certification should be verified. 7. Serving size of 120 g is estimated based on five footlongs (each ~360 g assembled) divided into 15 portions. 8. The platter price of SGD 69.00 is preserved from the field survey data source record for commercial context only and is not used as a nutritional evidence source.
